weho.one_line_summary: WeHo's lunch identity is plant-forward Mediterranean and chef-driven indulgence, with vegan-Mexican and Korean-bowl indies pulling against a steady stream of truffle-and-burrata fine dining.
weho.headline_signal: Truffle is the only flavor in WeHo with elite signal on both sides of the cross-source check (0.722 competitive / 0.722 leading, 87 + 98 mentions) — yet none of the three fast-casual chains can deliver it from pantry.
weho.top_signals_synopsis: Mole leads the competitive pool at 0.815 with 26 indie mentions but only 5 mentions in the leading-chain pool — it's an established Mexican-indie staple, not a chef trend, and quarterly velocity peaked back in 2024-Q3 then flattened. Truffle (0.722 / 0.722) and pesto (0.547 / 0.664) read very differently: pesto and miso (0.561 / 0.628) are slightly leading-skewed, meaning chains are already pushing them, while truffle is universal. Cauliflower (0.474 / 0.608) and burrata (0.357 / 0.591) are the clearest chef-led rising signals — leading-pool scores meaningfully exceed competitive, with truffle and burrata showing a noticeable 2025-Q4 to 2026-Q1 acceleration.

Chipotle.reason: Zero deliverable strengths against WeHo's top 8 — mole, truffle, pesto, miso, cauliflower, burrata, kimchi, and jerk are all missing-SKU gaps.

CAVA.reason: Only lentils (rank 15, score 0.275) intersects pantry; none of the top 8 WeHo signals are deliverable from current SKUs.

Sweetgreen.reason: Pesto (0.664) and miso (0.628) are both deliverable and in the top 8; kimchi (0.519) covers a second-tier signal, but truffle, cauliflower, and burrata are gaps.

key_opportunity.rationale: Miso is a top-4 WeHo signal (0.628 leading-pool, 19 mentions, peak trajectory with a 2026-Q1 spike to 9 mentions) and Sweetgreen already carries miso in pantry. Anchor a WeHo-only miso-forward bowl variant on top of the existing miso glazed salmon line — the SKU is in place, the local signal supports it.

key_constraint.rationale: Truffle is WeHo's only flavor where competitive and leading scores both clear 0.7 with combined 185 mentions, but no brand in the dataset carries truffle or any truffle-derived SKU. This is the defensible neighborhood gap.

recommended_actions: Ship-now: Sweetgreen WeHo — lean a seasonal LTO on the existing pesto + miso pantry; both signals are top-4 and already in SKU.
Test-now: Sweetgreen — add a truffle oil drizzle SKU at the WeHo store only; truffle is the highest-signal flavor in the city and no fast-casual is delivering it.

Defensive: Chipotle WeHo — mole is the #1 competitive signal at 0.815 and Chipotle has no path to it from pantry; either pilot a mole base SKU or concede the Mexican-indie lunch occasion to Gracias Madre and the taquerias.

ship_now.rationale: Build the bowl around achiote-marinated, pineapple-finished al pastor pork over cilantro-lime rice, black beans, and a charred-pineapple salsa. Al pastor carries an emerging signal in WeHo and it's the highest-ranked flavor Chipotle's existing pantry can authentically deliver here.
ship_now.signal_rank_note: At rank 16 this is not WeHo's top-trending flavor, but the highest-ranked one Chipotle can honestly ship from current pantry without inventing SKUs.

gap_fill.dish_potential: Adding a single mole-negra base SKU would unlock a slow-braised chicken mole bowl over rice with sesame and pickled onion, opening access to the city's very strong signal — the #1 flavor in WeHo by competitive score with 26 indie mentions.
gap_fill.comp_context: Gracias Madre's plant-based mole is the indie reference point WeHo diners are anchoring to.

ship_now.rationale: Center the bowl on slow-stewed red lentils with harissa, charred cauliflower from a different line, lemon-tahini, and pita crumb. Lentils sit at an emerging signal in WeHo and route directly through CAVA's existing lentil SKU without any flavor-gesturing.
ship_now.signal_rank_note: At rank 15 lentils are not WeHo's top-trending flavor, but they're the highest-ranked term CAVA can authentically deliver from current pantry.

gap_fill.dish_potential: A mole-base SKU would let CAVA ship a cross-cuisine mole-glazed chicken pita or grain bowl — unusual for the brand but unlocking the city's very strong #1 signal and differentiating from competing Mediterranean fast-casual.
gap_fill.comp_context: Gracias Madre's mole work is the local cultural reference; the indie scene in this neighborhood is driving the signal.

ship_now.rationale: Toss arugula and shredded kale with house basil pesto, roasted chickpeas, blistered tomato, shaved parmesan, and a lemon-pesto vinaigrette. Pesto reads as a strong signal in WeHo's leading pool and Sweetgreen already carries pesto in pantry.
ship_now.signal_rank_note: Pesto is rank 3 in WeHo — a top-tier flavor that Sweetgreen can ship today without any SKU investment.

gap_fill.dish_potential: Adding a mole base SKU would let Sweetgreen build a mole-glazed sweet potato or chicken bowl with cilantro-lime rice, unlocking the city's #1 very strong signal and giving Sweetgreen a defensible position no other fast-casual holds.
gap_fill.comp_context: Gracias Madre is the WeHo indie carrying the mole signal locally.

williamsburg.one_line_summary: Williamsburg's indie scene is Caribbean-led with a strong Israeli-Mediterranean second act, and chef pool and review pool agree more tightly here than in either other city.
williamsburg.headline_signal: Jerk is the strongest single-flavor signal in the entire dataset — 0.917 on both pools, 44 indie mentions plus 69 leading mentions — and not one of the three chains can deliver it; Sweetgreen's gap alone needs four SKUs (jerk paste, allspice, scotch bonnet, thyme).
williamsburg.top_signals_synopsis: Jerk towers over everything (0.917 / 0.917) and oxtail (0.500 / 0.617, 98 leading mentions) confirms the Caribbean identity is not just one Kokomo-driven artifact. Pesto (0.452 / 0.735) and miso (0.621 / 0.704) are leading-skewed peak signals — chains and chef-pool indies are both pushing these, but the leading edge is sharper, suggesting chef-driven momentum. Cauliflower (0.730 / 0.597) and kimchi (0.623 / 0.406) flip the pattern: these are established in the competitive indie set more than in the trend-leading pool, so they read as durable lunch staples rather than rising plays.

Chipotle.reason: Top 8 Williamsburg signals are jerk, pesto, cauliflower, miso, truffle, birria, kimchi, oxtail — Chipotle's pantry delivers none of them.

CAVA.reason: Only tahini (rank 14, 0.430) is a CAVA strength; none of the top 8 signals are deliverable, but tahini at least intersects the Israeli-indie second wave (K'Far, Laser Wolf).

Sweetgreen.reason: Three of the top 8 are pantry strengths: pesto (0.735), miso (0.704), and kimchi (0.623) — the strongest brand-city fit in the entire dataset.

key_opportunity.rationale: Pesto is the #2 Williamsburg signal at 0.735 leading-pool with 26 mentions, peak trajectory, and Sweetgreen already lists pesto in pantry plus a Chicken Pesto Parm dish on the existing menu. Extend the pesto base into a Williamsburg-specific LTO rather than re-inventing — the pantry, the menu precedent, and the local signal all line up.

key_constraint.rationale: Jerk is the strongest signal in any city in the dataset and is a gap for all three brands. Sweetgreen needs four SKUs to enter the space; Chipotle and CAVA need the same four. Anyone who builds the four-SKU jerk pantry first wins the Caribbean lunch occasion in Williamsburg outright.

recommended_actions: Ship-now: Sweetgreen Williamsburg — add a pesto + miso LTO bowl; both are top-4 signals, both are pantry-deliverable, and pesto already has menu precedent.

Test-now: Sweetgreen or CAVA — pilot the 4-SKU jerk pantry (jerk paste, allspice, scotch bonnet, thyme) at the Williamsburg store; this is the single highest-signal flavor we measured anywhere and currently uncontested by fast-casual.

Defensive: Chipotle Williamsburg — birria sits at 0.499 / 0.682 with 19 + 40 mentions and is a four-SKU gap (birria, consommé, guajillo, ancho); without a birria entry, Chipotle cedes the trending-Mexican lunch to the indie pool.

ship_now.dish_concept: Al Pastor Burrito with Charred Pineapple
ship_now.rationale: Spit-style achiote al pastor pork with charred pineapple, red onion, cilantro, and chipotle crema, wrapped or bowled. Al pastor carries a background signal in Williamsburg but it's the only top-25 flavor Chipotle can honestly deliver from current pantry without inventing SKUs.
ship_now.signal_rank_note: At rank 21 this is not Williamsburg's top-trending flavor, but the highest-ranked one Chipotle can authentically ship today.

gap_fill.dish_potential: A single pesto SKU would let Chipotle launch a pesto-lime cilantro rice or a basil-pesto chicken bowl LTO — a cross-cuisine move that captures Williamsburg's #2 strong signal with one ingredient add.
gap_fill.comp_context: K'Far and the Israeli-Mediterranean indies are pushing herb-forward sauces; the indie scene in this neighborhood is driving the signal.

ship_now.rationale: Smoke-charred eggplant over tahini-yogurt, with pickled cabbage, herbs, and za'atar pita. Tahini carries a notable signal in Williamsburg and routes directly through CAVA's existing tahini SKU — exactly the Israeli-indie lane K'Far and Laser Wolf occupy.
ship_now.signal_rank_note: At rank 14 tahini isn't Williamsburg's top-trending flavor, but it's the highest-ranked one CAVA can authentically deliver and aligns tightly with the local Israeli identity.

gap_fill.dish_potential: A pesto SKU would let CAVA ship a pesto-tahini grain bowl or pesto-yogurt drizzle line, unlocking Williamsburg's #2 strong signal with a single-ingredient add that complements the existing Mediterranean grammar.
gap_fill.comp_context: The Israeli and Italian-leaning indies in Williamsburg are driving the herb-sauce signal in this neighborhood.

ship_now.rationale: Extend the existing Chicken Pesto Parm format into a Williamsburg-only bowl: house pesto, shaved parmesan, charred broccolini, farro, and lemon-pesto dressing. Pesto reads as a strong signal in Williamsburg's leading pool and Sweetgreen carries pesto in pantry with menu precedent.
ship_now.signal_rank_note: Pesto is rank 2 in Williamsburg — a top-tier flavor Sweetgreen can ship today with zero SKU investment.

gap_fill.dish_potential: Adding cauliflower as a roasted-veg SKU would unlock a charred-cauliflower harissa bowl or cauliflower-miso line, capturing Williamsburg's #3 strong signal with one ingredient — and it sits adjacent to existing Shroomami-style preparations.
gap_fill.comp_context: The competitive indie pool in Williamsburg has cauliflower at 27 mentions; the indie scene in this neighborhood is driving the signal.

mission.one_line_summary: The Mission is a Mexican-burrito institution layered with high-end fusion, putting concentrated pressure on Chipotle specifically and rewarding craft mole and birria over chain-style fillings.
mission.headline_signal: Birria is mentioned 159 times in the competitive indie pool (0.669) versus 17 in the leading pool — the largest indie-vs-chef divergence in the entire dataset, and it's a four-SKU gap (birria, consommé, guajillo, ancho) for every brand.
mission.top_signals_synopsis: Mole leads at 0.865 / 0.865 with 114 + 53 mentions and a sustained 2025-Q1 through 2026-Q1 plateau of 20+ mentions per quarter — this is a structural Mission signal, not a moment. Birria (0.669 / 0.452) and al pastor (0.585 / 0.102) are deeply competitive-pool-skewed: they are taqueria staples, not chef trends, and they define the lunch occasion Chipotle competes in. Miso (0.528 / 0.778) and truffle (0.269 / 0.585, rising) are leading-pool plays driven by Lazy Bear / Foreign Cinema / Tartine — distinct chef signal, much less corroboration in the indie pool. Pesto (0.688 / 0.688) is the rare both-sides peak with even split.

Chipotle.reason: Al pastor (0.585, rank 5) is a Chipotle pantry strength and the Mission's strongest Mexican-staple signal beyond mole; but mole, birria, miso, and pesto in the top 4 are all gaps.

CAVA.reason: Only lentils (0.313, rank 15) intersects — none of the top 8 Mission signals deliver from CAVA's current pantry; the Mission's Mexican-forward identity is the worst CAVA fit of the three cities.

Sweetgreen.reason: Miso (0.778) and pesto (0.688) are top-3 signals and both pantry strengths; kimchi (0.461) covers a second-tier signal, but mole, birria, al pastor, and truffle are gaps.

key_opportunity.rationale: Al pastor is rank 5 at 0.585 with 69 indie mentions and steady 2025-Q3 through 2026-Q2 quarterly velocity (8-11 per quarter). Chipotle already has al pastor in pantry — Mission-only menu prominence (LTO callout, premium upcharge, signage referencing taqueria-style preparation) is a near-zero-cost defense of the Mission burrito occasion.

key_constraint.rationale: Mole is the highest cross-source signal in the Mission with 114 competitive mentions and 53 leading mentions, sustained quarter over quarter, and no brand carries mole in pantry. This is the structural gap that defines why La Taqueria and El Farolito out-position chain Mexican here.

recommended_actions: Ship-now: Chipotle Mission — feature al pastor as the headline protein in a Mission-only LTO; it's pantry-ready and the #5 city signal at 0.585.
Test-now: Chipotle or Sweetgreen Mission — pilot a single mole-base SKU; mole is the city's #1 signal at 0.865 and is uncontested by every fast-casual in the dataset.

Defensive: Sweetgreen Mission — birria pressure is at 0.669 with 159 indie mentions; without a four-SKU birria entry (birria, consommé, guajillo, ancho), Sweetgreen's lunch share erodes to the taquerias before any salad-occasion play matters.

ship_now.rationale: Lead with achiote-marinated al pastor pork finished with grilled pineapple, white onion, cilantro, and a roasted-tomato salsa — burrito or bowl, with a premium taqueria-style callout. Al pastor reads as a strong signal in the Mission's competitive indie pool and Chipotle already carries it in pantry.
ship_now.signal_rank_note: Al pastor is rank 5 in the Mission — a top-tier flavor Chipotle can defend today with menu prominence rather than any new SKU.

gap_fill.dish_potential: Adding a mole-negra base SKU would let Chipotle ship a slow-simmered chicken or pork mole burrito — capturing the Mission's #1 very strong signal and giving Chipotle its first credible answer to La Taqueria's mole-based positioning.
gap_fill.comp_context: La Taqueria and El Farolito anchor the mole signal in the Mission, with 114 indie mentions concentrated in that taqueria pool.

ship_now.dish_concept: Smoked Red Lentil and Pickled Onion Pita
ship_now.rationale: Lead with slow-cooked smoked red lentils, charred onion, pickled cabbage, sumac yogurt, and crispy chickpeas in a warm pita. Lentils carry an emerging signal in the Mission and route through CAVA's existing lentil SKU without flavor-gesturing.
ship_now.signal_rank_note: At rank 15 lentils are not the Mission's top-trending flavor, but they're the highest-ranked term CAVA can authentically deliver from current pantry in this Mexican-heavy market.

gap_fill.dish_potential: A mole-base SKU would let CAVA build a Mediterranean-meets-Mexican mole-spiced lamb or chicken pita — an unusual but defensible cross-cuisine move that unlocks the Mission's #1 very strong signal and differentiates CAVA from every fast-casual operator in the city.
gap_fill.comp_context: La Taqueria and El Farolito drive the mole signal in this neighborhood; the taqueria indie pool is sustaining it quarter over quarter.

ship_now.dish_concept: Miso-Glazed Salmon and Crispy Rice Bowl
ship_now.rationale: Extend the existing Miso Glazed Salmon line into a Mission-specific bowl with crispy short-grain rice, charred broccolini, pickled cucumber, and a miso-ginger dressing. Miso carries a very strong signal in the Mission's leading pool and Sweetgreen already carries miso plus a Miso Glazed Salmon dish.
ship_now.signal_rank_note: Miso is rank 2 in the Mission — a top-tier flavor Sweetgreen can ship today with full pantry and menu precedent in place.

gap_fill.dish_potential: A mole-base SKU would let Sweetgreen build a mole-glazed sweet potato or grain-bowl LTO — unlocking the Mission's #1 very strong signal and giving Sweetgreen a defensible cross-cuisine position no other fast-casual holds in the burrito-defined Mission lunch.
gap_fill.comp_context: La Taqueria and El Farolito are the taqueria institutions sustaining the mole signal here, with 114 indie mentions concentrated in the Mission's competitive pool.
